directions.name: Retune The Active One-K 128x128 Hot-Band Copy Cadence
directions.hypothesis: Round 16/50 still has no extra user-authored idea family in `state/human_review.md` beyond the approval gate and the exactly-one-direction rule, so the next move should follow the latest measured evidence as narrowly as possible. The current source surface already matches the recorded best-custom kernel source in `src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu` (no kernel diff between `1181247` and `e6fdb8b`), and the latest NCU capture shows the dominant `128x128` hot-band kernel is the only branch that is even slightly behind that best reference: hot-band time is `32,917,184 ns` now versus `32,868,864 ns` on `1181247`, while the peeled and tail kernels are not slower in the current capture. The remaining gap is narrow and still looks like feed/shared-stage friction inside the active one-K loop (`7.73%` long-scoreboard now versus `7.49%` on `1181247`, `13.02%` DRAM versus `12.84%`, with `196` regs/thread and only `16.64%` active warps), so the best next move is a bounded retune of the current K16 copy/wait cadence and B-stage handoff instead of another family pivot.
directions.expected_bottleneck: A narrow hot-band feed-latency and shared-stage handoff gap inside the active one-K `128x128` kernel, not the peeled `64x384` residual rows or the `64x96` tail.
directions.risk: Low to moderate. This stays on the current best-known source family and only retunes the active hot-band loop, but the measured gap versus `1181247` is already small, so the upside is bounded and an over-aggressive wait/order change can easily erase the recovered win.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the current `25.325055 ms` run and the recorded best `24.422464 ms`
hot-band `gpu__time_duration.sum` versus the current `32,917,184 ns` and the `1181247` reference `32,868,864 ns`
directions.stop_condition: Stop if a first-pass cadence/order retune cannot beat the current `25.325055 ms` control run or if the hot-band kernel stays within noise of the current `32,917,184 ns` capture without improving long-scoreboard or tensor activity.

directions.name: Reopen The PTX One-K 128x128 Hot-Band Control Branch
directions.hypothesis: Because `state/human_review.md` still requires exactly one approved direction at a time and does not add a competing manual family, keep the PTX one-K branch as a clean fallback rather than mixing it with the active-loop retune. The latest run shows the recovered standard one-K `128x128` branch is healthy but still just off the best reference. The PTX one-K control branch remains relevant because it preserves the same grouped-row `128x128` geometry while swapping the hot-band accumulate/control path, and its prior measured run `20260420_191909_bf16_gemm_v1_00b6a0a` reached `25.379312 ms`, close enough to stay in play if the active K16 path has saturated.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band inner-loop control and accumulate/export behavior in the standard one-K `128x128` branch, addressed by the PTX control path rather than by changing CTA geometry.
directions.risk: Moderate to high. The PTX WMMA path is correctness-sensitive, and its last measured result was competitive but still slower than both the current control run and the recorded best custom run.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the current `25.325055 ms` control run, the prior PTX `25.379312 ms` run, and the recorded best `24.422464 ms`
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the PTX one-K control branch cannot beat the current recovered standard branch cleanly or if it still stays above the prior `25.379312 ms` PTX result.

directions.name: Promote The Existing 256x128 Pivot Hot-Band Kernel
directions.hypothesis: With no extra human-review idea family queued, the third direction should be a materially different hot-band family rather than a return to stale cleanup work. The active recovered branch still launches the main hot band as a four-warp `128x128` CTA with `196` regs/thread, `22,016` B shared/block, register/shared occupancy caps of `2/2`, and only `16.64%` active warps. The in-tree `256x128` pivot kernel is the cleanest remaining way to test whether CTA geometry and block-count overhead, not just K16 cadence, are the limit on the first `6400 x 7680` rows. It halves the pivot-band CTA count while restoring an eight-warp CTA, which is materially different from both the active one-K branch and the PTX control variant.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band CTA geometry and block-count overhead in the current four-warp `128x128` path, rather than a pure copy-cadence issue.
directions.risk: Moderate. The `256x128` pivot family already exists, but it has not been the accepted default on the restored surface, and a larger CTA can still lose if it trades CTA-count relief for a worse live-set or export footprint.
metrics_to_recheck: hot-band kernel name and grid size in `ncu_metrics.csv` to confirm the pivot CTA-count change
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the `256x128` pivot family cannot beat the current one-K `128x128` control run or if it reintroduces the kind of wide-family regression already seen when the stale `64x384` route was reopened.

notes: Round 16/50 human-review audit: `state/human_review.md` still contributes only the approval/selection gate and the exactly-one-direction rule, with no extra user-authored idea family to prioritize. Accepted as the primary family for this round: bounded in-family tuning on the active one-K `128x128` hot-band path, because the current kernel source already matches the recorded best-custom `1181247` source in `src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu`, and the latest NCU evidence says only the dominant hot-band kernel is slightly behind that best reference (`32,917,184 ns` now versus `32,868,864 ns` on `1181247`, with `7.73%` versus `7.49%` long-scoreboard and `13.02%` versus `12.84%` DRAM). Deferred fallback families: the PTX one-K `128x128` control branch and the existing `256x128` pivot hot-band branch, both kept as single-direction A/B paths that still satisfy the human-review gate. Rejected as the primary family for this round: reopening the two-K `128x128x32` stage-deepening path, because the latest recovery came directly from removing it; also rejected again as a primary move is the stale sweep-backed full-band `64x384` family from `state/autotune_round18_main_tiles.md`, because direct node_a evidence on the restored surface already showed that reopening it regressed badly to `34.016768 ms`.
